channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Abhijeet Dipke thanks Uddhav Thackeray for supporting CJP and Wangchuk's protest over exam paper leaks
item.description: Abhijeet Dipke, founder of the Cockroach Janata Party, has publicly thanked Shiv Sena (UBT) chief Uddhav Thackeray for his support to the CJP (Congress of Progressive Journalists) and activist Sonam W...

item.title: Ibis Ikeja Hotel Owner Prefers Expatriates Over Nigerians for Management Roles
item.description: Femi Okenla, owner of Ibis Ikeja Hotel, has sparked debate by stating he prefers expatriates over Nigerians for senior management roles. He cited concerns about work ethic and trust, sharing an incide...

item.title: Tanzania's Strategies for Brain Cancer Treatment
item.description: Tanzania is developing specialized protocols to address brain cancer, a complex disease requiring advanced medical resources. Dr. Nchimbi, a senior health official, highlighted the country's efforts t...

item.title: Major Banks Report Strong Earnings Growth in Q2
item.description: The Wall Street Journal's live coverage highlights the robust financial performance of major banks in the second quarter of 2026. Leading institutions reported significant revenue increases, driven by...

item.title: Calgary ARC Approves $16.4 Billion Shell Acquisition
item.description: The Calgary-based Alberta Renewable Capital (ARC) has approved a landmark $16.4 billion merger with Royal Dutch Shell, marking a significant shift in Canada's energy sector. This deal, which gained ap...

item.title: DepEd Plans Sports Academies to Redirect Youth from Computer Games
item.description: The Department of Education (DepEd) is planning to establish more sports academies to channel the energy of youth away from computer games, following recent crime incidents involving students. Educati...

item.title: Historical Football Rivalry Between England and Argentina
item.description: The article explores the enduring football rivalry between England and Argentina, tracing its roots back to the 19th century. It highlights key historical matches, including the 1966 World Cup quarter...

item.title: New York Imposes Moratorium on Large Data Centers Amid Environmental Concerns
item.description: New York has become the first U.S. state to impose a one-year moratorium on large new data centers, citing concerns over rising utility bills, strained water resources, and community burdens. Governor...

item.title: Urban planning gaps in Melbourne's access to natural spaces compared to Sydney
item.description: The article highlights the disparity in access to natural spaces between Melbourne and Sydney, focusing on the challenges faced by Melbourne's urban planning. It discusses how advocacy groups opposing...

item.title: DSWD Expands Cash Grants for 4Ps Beneficiaries Under First 1,000 Days Program
item.description: The Department of Social Welfare and Development (DSWD) has expanded its First 1,000 Days (F1KD) cash grant program, increasing monthly benefits for qualified Pantawid Pamilyang Pilipino Program (4Ps)...

item.title: U.S. Orders Strikes Against Iran After Attacks on Hormuz Ships
item.description: U.S. President Donald Trump authorized additional strikes against Iranian military targets in response to Iran's attacks on commercial vessels in the Strait of Hormuz. The U.S. Central Command confirm...

item.title: Police Arrest PFIPC DG Adeniyi Adeyemi After Federal High Court Issues Bench Warrant
item.description: Adeniyi Adeyemi, the Director-General of the controversial Presidential Foreign Intervention Promotion Council (PFIPC), has been arrested in Osun State after a Federal High Court in Abuja ordered secu...

item.title: Semaglutide May Slow Biological Aging in HIV Patients, Study Suggests
item.description: Researchers from the University of California San Diego and collaborating institutions found that semaglutide, the active ingredient in weight-loss drugs like Ozempic and Wegovy, may slow biological a...

item.title: EPRA Retains Fuel Prices for July–August as Government Extends Tax Relief and Fuel Subsidy
item.description: The Energy and Petroleum Regulatory Authority (EPRA) has announced that pump prices for Super Petrol, Diesel and Kerosene will remain unchanged across Kenya for the pricing period running from 15 July...

item.title: Acting Attorney General Todd Blanche's oversight of politically charged DOJ investigations under scrutiny
item.description: Newly released emails and documents reveal Acting Attorney General Todd Blanche's extensive involvement in overseeing politically motivated investigations within the Department of Justice (DOJ). The m...

item.title: Life Sentence for Ex-KDF Major Who Killed Wife and Two Children
item.description: Former Nairobi governor Mike Sonko has praised the life sentence given to ex-KDF Major Peter Mugure, who was convicted of murdering his wife and two children. The High Court in Nyeri sentenced Mugure ...

item.title: EU imposes gold import ban on Sudan to disrupt conflict funding
item.description: The European Union has imposed a comprehensive ban on gold imports from Sudan to cut off funding for the ongoing civil war. This measure targets the trade of Sudanese gold, which has been a critical r...

item.title: Parliamentary Committee Finds No Criminal Links for Senzo Mchunu, Raises Governance Concerns
item.description: The ad hoc parliamentary committee investigating allegations of criminal infiltration into South Africa's criminal justice system has concluded that there is no evidence to support claims of criminal ...

item.title: Spiritual leader's brother and three others charged with assault in land dispute
item.description: A case was registered against Shaligram Garg, the younger brother of spiritual leader Dhirendra Shastri, and three associates for allegedly assaulting a man over a land dispute in Chhatarpur district....

item.title: England and Argentina World Cup Semi-Final Referee Pick Sparks Debate
item.description: FIFA has appointed American referee Ismail Elfath to officiate the England vs Argentina World Cup 2026 semi-final on July 15. Elfath, a seasoned MLS referee, will be supported by assistants Corey Park...
